SOS,London Borough of Hackney, London Fields E8 The London Fields Boys (LF) are a large street gang based on estates situated around the London Fields

E8. The gangs main territory is the estates adjoining the west side of the London Fields like the Blackstone, Fields and Morland estates. The area is also referred to as "Fields" and "Mashtown". The older generation of LFB are also known as 'Mashtown', whilst the current younger generation from the London Fields area are known as 'Zero Tolerance' (ZT) and 'Chest Shot Gang' (CSG). The 'London Fields Boys' had originally made up the largest part of an older gang known as the 'Hackney Boys', up until the late 1990s and early 2000s. 'London Fields Boys' were a significant part of the 'Hackney Boys', and were most active in their rivalry with the 'Tottenham Mandem' (TMD) between 1996-2003. Although the 'Hackney Boys' were mainly drawn from London Fields they would also draw membership from estates around Hackney Downs, Haggerston and Well street covering the E5, E8 and E9 postcodes. By the late 2000s the older members of the 'London Fields Boys'

'Hackney Boys' had began to move into music production forming their own group known as "Mashtown". The "Mashtown" group is also closely linked to the 'London Fields Boys' as were a younger generation known as 'Ride Out Squad' (ROS). Following the Tottenham and Hackney gang wars of the late 1990s and early 2000s the younger generations formed new smaller gangs that had become disaggregated from the old larger gangs such as the 'Hackney Boys' and the 'Tottenham Mandem'. Whilst 'London Fields Boys' were descended from the 'Hackney Boys', their nearby rivals the 'Holly Street Boys' were descended from a group known as the 'Rowdy Bunch', and other nearby rivals 'Hoxton Boys' were descended from another group known as 'Love of Money' (LOM).  The rivalry between London Fields and Holly Street is one of the oldest and most reputed gang rivalries in the city having now been ongoing since the 1990s. There is still bitter rivalry between the older London Fields Boys and older Tottenham Mandem who were active in the Hackney and Tottenham conflict. On rare occasions the bitter personal disputes can still result in incidents. One occurred in 2009 at the Urban Music Awards which involved "Mashtown" (London Fields) and "NorthStar" (Tottenham) at the O2 Arena. 'Tottenham Mandem' leaders Clint 'C1' Ponton and 'Wes Lambie' of the "NorthStar" group were attacked with a champagne bottle during the event. It came as retaliation as they were accused of having gunned down the younger brother of "Mashtown" artist Margs. Over the years the historical Tottenham-Hackney gang wars have faded and the current younger generations from both areas London Fields (ZT) in Hackney and Broadwater Farm (OFB) in Tottenham are now loosely affiliated with each other. London Fields are allied with EC1, Stoke Newington, A-Road, Mountford, Kingsmead, Gilpin Square, Red Pitch, and Hindle House, whilst the older generation also have links with other areas, such as the Wood Green, Edmonton, Stonebridge and South Kilburn areas. During the later 2000s into early 2010s the younger generation known as the Ride Out Squad (ROS) also had ties with Beckton and the E3 Bloods (Roman Road and Devons Road). They are also allied with RPG, Gilpin Square, Kingsmead, Fellows, Stokey, A-Road and Mountford. Fields have beef with Holly Street, Hoxton, Homerton, Kingshold, Pembury, Stamford Hill, Manor House, Essex Road and Cally Road.  The younger generations beef with EC1 but the older generation are still allied with EC1. The new generation are known to flag Black more than Blue, and also go by the name SOS
Shoot On Sight. Associated Rappers} Asco

rappers: Rash @manlikeilyasss IG "GEOMETRYCOLLECTION (), ))", CT1 (71st),London Borough of Camden, Camden Town NW1 CT1 (CrackTown1st) are a street gang who are based around Camden Town within the NW1 postcode, known locally as 'Crack 1st'. They are active around Camden High Street and surrounding area including the Curnock, Ampthill, Camden Street, Bayham Place, Gilbey's Yard and Three Fields estates. They are a predominantly Somali gang and are the current generation of ''Camden Malis'', following on from older gangs like TMS, ANC and Centric. The ''Camden Malis'' were known as the 'Centric Crew' in the late 1990s, claiming the estate around Oval Road and Gilbey's Yard as their main base. The gang took their name from Centric Close, a small street which adjoins Oval Road. The gang came into conflict with the 'North London Somalians' (NLS), from the Turnpike Lane area of North London. 'Centric Crew' had later become referred to as the 'African Nations Crew' (ANC) to reflect the fact their membership was drawn from a number of African countries, and during late 2007

08 their younger generation, including the younger relatives of the olders, had earned the name 'The Money Squad' (TMS) The TMS gang would go on to dominate the drug trade around Camden Town and Kings Cross, being reported regularly by local press during the late 2000s and the 2010s. Many members of 'CT1' also rep 'Active Gxng' who are a larger and more well known set in the area. They are allied with 'Active Gxng', 'FSG' and 'NRB' in an alliance known as '71st' after the N7 and NW1 postcodes where they are based. Their main rivals are 'Cumbo', 'Peckwater' and 'Queens Crescent' who form an alliance known as '51st' after the NW5 and NW1 postcodes where they are based. Associated Rappers} 2Smokeyy (Rocky)

N15 TPL, which stands for 'Turnpike Lane' or 'Touch Paper Lane' and 'Take People's Lives', are a street gang situated around the Turnpike Lane and Ducketts Common area, drawing the members from the N8, N15 and N22 postcodes. TPL are of predominantly Somali and Albanian origin. TPL was created through the merger of two local street gangs during the mid 2000s. The local Somali gang were known as 'North London Somalians' (NLS), and the 'Young Militant Somalians' (YMS) The Albanian side were known as 'Albanian Young Gunners' (AYG) 'Young Albanian Gangsters' (YAG) and 'Real Albanian Gangsters' (RAG). In the late 2010s the two TPL sets would start to come into conflict and are now rivals. The Somali dominated set are now known as DC8 (Duckets Common 8) and the YMS (Young Militant Somalians). Their members are mainly drawn from the N8 postcode area. The gang are known to identify with the colour of blue. They are allied with nearby Hornsey
